Android detached-agent lifecycle fix — AFTER-fix verification on emulator
=========================================================================
Fix under test : commit 4ed10049281
                 "fix(android): keep detached agent alive across non-explicit
                  service teardown"
Source of truth: packages/app-core/platforms/android/app/src/main/java/ai/elizaos/app/ElizaAgentService.java
                 (ai.elizaos.app builds the canonical app-core tree, not
                  packages/app/android — androidUsesAppDirFor("ai.elizaos.app")==false)
Device         : emulator-5584 (sdk_gphone64_arm64, Android 15 / API 35, 1440x2960, 3 GB RAM)

BEFORE (already committed in this dir, buggy build):
  smoke-run1-agent-killed.txt / agent-destroy-race-logcat.txt / agent-destroy-race-diagnostics.jsonl
  onDestroy always called stopAgentProcess(); an FGS-denial / AMS teardown that
  fired while the agent was cold-booting killed the freshly-launched bun
  (code=143), and the Android-15 background FGS-start denial meant nothing
  restarted it -> local mode stayed dead. Harness SIGTERM'd at 190/240 "not
  healthy: fetch failed".

BUILD (fixed):
  bun run --cwd packages/app build:android  ->  BUILD SUCCESSFUL (android-build.log)
  APK: packages/app-core/.../apk/debug/app-debug.apk  (mtime 17:07, after the 16:40 source fix)
  Fix markers confirmed compiled into shipped APK classes18.dex (agent-fix-build-verification.txt):
    service-destroy-preserved-agent / "leaving the agent process running for adoption" /
    detached-agent-cold-boot-guard / detachedLaunchStartedAtMs / "Service destroyed without a stop request"
  Installed over the buggy build (adb install -r), lastUpdateTime 17:09:38.

AFTER — PROOF 1 (natural LOW_MEMORY process-death survival + recovery)
  file: agent-lmk-survival-harness-logcat.txt  (live run of the exact before-run harness:
        node scripts/mobile-local-chat-smoke.mjs --platform android --require-installed --live
        --android-select-local --android-stage-smoke-model  -> exit 0)
  - 17:14:14  "Process ai.elizaos.app (pid 5232) has died: fg +50 FGS"  (reason LOW_MEMORY,
              a non-explicit teardown — the class the fix targets)
  - the detached bun agent (a SEPARATE process, pid 6516, PPID=1/init) SURVIVED the app
    process death and kept LISTENING on 127.0.0.1:31337 (/proc/net/tcp state 0A + ESTABLISHED conns)
  - service auto-restarted (pid 6354); 17:15:13 "Detached agent health check passed."
  - harness reached: health {ready:true}, "Android process stable: 3 consecutive healthy
    samples", "Local inference ready via hub-active."   (before-run: never healthy, killed)
  - the before-run smoking gun is ABSENT: no "Agent process exited early ... code=143",
    no "Stopping agent process", no "stop-detached-agent" in the after run.
  Also captured the exact FGS-denial trigger during a relaunch:
    17:28:49 "Bringing down service while still waiting for start foreground: ...ElizaAgentService"
    -> agent NOT killed (fix's foregroundStartDenied exclusion).

AFTER — PROOF 2 (deterministic AMS service-teardown-without-process-death: the preserve branch)
  files: agent-preserved-after-fix-logcat.txt + agent-preserved-after-fix-diagnostics.jsonl
  Sequence (app kept FOREGROUND/TOP so LMK can't kill the process):
    17:36:58  START_AGENT FGS Allowed, startForegroundDelayMs:29094  (agent cold-boot window)
    17:37:03  ElizaAgent(9380): "Agent launcher started"            (detached bun pid 9631 spawned)
    17:37:27  adb am stopservice ai.elizaos.app/.ElizaAgentService  (non-explicit teardown, NOT ACTION_STOP)
    17:37:42  ElizaAgent(9380): "Service destroyed without a stop request; leaving the agent
              process running for adoption."
    17:37:54  ElizaAgent(9380): "Detached agent still in cold boot (launched 51081ms ago);
              not relaunching."   (the restart's cold-boot-guard leaves the still-booting bun alone)
  Diagnostics (agent-restart-diagnostics.jsonl) — BOTH new events the task asked to grep for:
    {"event":"service-onDestroy",...,"details":{"shuttingDown":"false",...,"detachedAgentMode":"true"}}
    {"event":"service-destroy-preserved-agent",...,"details":{"shuttingDown":"false",...}}
    {"event":"detached-agent-cold-boot-guard",...,"details":{"ageMs":"51080","bootGraceMs":"120000"}}
  Process check: bun pid PRE-teardown = 9631, bun pid POST-teardown = 9631  (IDENTICAL == PRESERVED),
  and still alive after the restart's cold-boot-guard (not relaunched/killed).
  In the before-code this same onDestroy path would have run stopAgentProcess() and killed bun 9631.

CONCLUSION: the fix resolves the race on the emulator. A non-explicit service teardown
(LOW_MEMORY process death OR AMS service teardown OR FGS-denial) no longer kills the detached
agent; it is preserved and adopted, and local mode reaches healthy instead of staying dead.

NOTE on screenshots: this 3 GB arm64 emulator is CPU/RAM-saturated while the detached bun
loads the 128k-context local model (repeated app + system_server ANRs, "Process system isn't
responding"). The WebView UI shell blocks on the local agent becoming healthy, so full
per-view screenshots (Messages/Settings/Wallet/...) could not be captured reliably here — an
environmental hardware limit, not a code fault. Captured: launcher/splash + cold-boot ANR
frames (screenshots/). The lifecycle fix — this lane's deliverable — is fully verified above.
